one_line: The financial system explained in 30 seconds — how money, debt, and wealth actually work, and why nobody teaches you this.
pillars: Psychology of Money (why smart people make dumb money decisions)
How Money Really Moves (banks, credit, the Fed, the invisible machinery)
Debt & Interest Mechanics (compound interest, credit scores, debt traps)
Investing Explainers (stocks, index funds, crypto, real estate, simplified)
Scams & Financial Traps (MLMs, payday loans, get-rich-quick schemes)
audience: People who feel confused or anxious about money, young adults navigating first jobs/credit cards/debt, and anyone interested in the financial literacy nobody taught them in school.
tone: Direct, myth-busting educator. Calm but urgent — like someone finally explaining the thing nobody told you.
hook_templates: This is what happens if you carry {object} for 10 years.
{object} is designed to keep you {adjective}. Here's how.

What's really happening when you {place}? The answer will shock you.
{object} could cost you {scale} over your lifetime. Here's the math.
In {scale}, {event}. This is what it means for your money.
Nobody explains {object} in school. And that's the scary part.
The financial system doesn't want you to think about what happens when you {place}.
{object} is bigger than you think. Here's the real number.
What if {event}? Here's what happens to your money.
There's {object} eating your paycheck and you don't see it.
This one decision about {object} determines your financial future.
